FIGURE_3_SPARCC_SPECIES

Published

April 11, 2026

https://biovcnet.github.io/_pages/NetworkScience_SparCC.nb.html

https://www.reddit.com/r/bioinformatics/comments/9d1vc0/anyone_have_experience_with_sparcc_are_there/

https://rachaellappan.github.io/16S-analysis/correlation-between-otus-with-sparcc.html#running-sparcc

https://www.annualreviews.org/content/journals/10.1146/annurev-statistics-042720-124436

PREPARE

Show the code 
library(tidyverse) 
library(SpiecEasi) 
library(otuSummary)
library(reshape2) 
library(psych)
Show the code 
## Helper functions
# Get lower triangle of the correlation matrix
  get_lower_tri<-function(cormat){
    cormat[upper.tri(cormat)] <- NA
    return(cormat)
  }
  # Get upper triangle of the correlation matrix
  get_upper_tri <- function(cormat){
    cormat[lower.tri(cormat)]<- NA
    return(cormat)
  }

First, species were filtered out in the data of all vaginal samples based on:

  1. prevalence <10%;

  2. mean relative abundance < 1‰;

  3. in <3 samples with a relative abundance lower than 1‰.

Next, in the data of each time-point, a species would be filtered out if its relative abundance had less than five non-zero values

https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-024-52102-1

Import

Show the code 
df_species <- read.delim("baby_species.tsv")
names(df_species)[1] <- "Sample"
library(readxl)
df_clinic <- read_excel("df_clinic_final.xlsx")
df_clinic <- as.data.frame(df_clinic)
row.names(df_clinic) <- df_clinic$Sample
Show the code 
df_species <- df_species[df_species$Sample %in% df_clinic$Sample, ]
Show the code 
row.names(df_species) <- df_species$Sample

df_species <- df_species[row.names(df_clinic), ]

species <- df_species[ , -1]
Show the code 
# SPECIES LÀ FILE RELATIVE ABUNDANCE (SUM SPECIES TRONG MOI SAMPLE LA 1)
sum(species[1,])
[1] 100
Show the code 
apply(species,
      MARGIN = 2,
      FUN = mean) -> check1

# head(check1)

mean(species[ , 1])
[1] 8.525365
Show the code 
# PHUONG AN LOC RA CAC SPECIES CO MEAN RELATIVE ABUNDANCE > 0.1 %
check1[check1 > 0.1] -> check2

# length(check2)

df_all <- species[ , which(colnames(species) %in% names(check2))]

SPARCC

Show the code 
set.seed(2026)
out <- SpiecEasi:::sparcc(df_all)

rownames(out$Cor) <- colnames(df_all)
colnames(out$Cor) <- colnames(df_all)
# out$Cor

rownames(out$Cov) <- colnames(df_all)
colnames(out$Cov) <- colnames(df_all)
# out$Cov
Show the code 
plotableSparcc <- out$Cor %>% 
  get_upper_tri() %>% 
  reshape2::melt() %>% na.omit()

Sparcc_plot <- plotableSparcc %>% ggplot(aes(x = Var2,
                                             y = Var1, 
                                             fill = value)) + 
  geom_tile() + 
  scale_fill_gradient2() + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1))

# Sparcc_plot

p-value by boostraping

Show the code 
library(foreach)
library(doParallel)

# How many cores does your CPU have
n_cores <- detectCores()
n_cores
[1] 16
Show the code 
cluster <- makeCluster(n_cores - 5)
registerDoParallel(cluster)

tp0 <- proc.time()
out2 <- sparccboot(df_all,
                   R = 10, # TANG GIAM O DAY DE RA P-VALUE CHINH XAC
                   ncpus = 7)
tp1 <- proc.time()
tp1 - tp0
   user  system elapsed 
  10.05    0.43   10.53
Show the code 
stopCluster(cl = cluster)

saveRDS(out2, "out2_SPCIES.rds")

out2 <- readRDS("out2_SPCIES.rds")
Show the code 
set.seed(2026)

outP <- pval.sparccboot(out2)

# outP

df_pvalue <- data.frame(outP$cors, outP$pvals)

# df_pvalue
Show the code 
cors <- outP$cors

pvals <- outP$pvals

sparCCpcors <- diag(0.5, nrow = dim(out$Cor)[1], ncol = dim(out$Cor)[1])

sparCCpcors[upper.tri(sparCCpcors, diag=FALSE)] <- cors

sparCCpcors <- sparCCpcors + t(sparCCpcors)

sparCCpval <- diag(0.5, nrow = dim(out$Cor)[1], ncol = dim(out$Cor)[1])
sparCCpval[upper.tri(sparCCpval, diag=FALSE)] <- pvals
sparCCpval <- sparCCpval + t(sparCCpval)

rownames(sparCCpcors) <- colnames(df_all)
colnames(sparCCpcors) <- colnames(df_all)
rownames(sparCCpval) <-  colnames(df_all)
colnames(sparCCpval) <-  colnames(df_all)

# sparCCpcors

# sparCCpval
Show the code 
sparccCor_processed <- sparCCpcors  %>% get_upper_tri() %>%
  reshape2::melt() %>% na.omit() %>% rename(cor = value)

sparccP_processed <- sparCCpval  %>% get_upper_tri() %>%
  reshape2::melt() %>% na.omit() %>% rename(p = value)

# join the two data frames

SparccP <- merge(x = sparccCor_processed, 
                 y = sparccP_processed, 
                 all = T,
                 by = c("Var1", "Var2")) %>%
  # calculate the false discovery rate to adjust for multiple p values
  mutate(fdr = p.adjust(p, method = "BH"))
Show the code 
fdrThresh <- 0.01

sparccOkP <- SparccP %>% filter(fdr < fdrThresh) 

png(filename = "sparcc_species.png",
    width = 20,
    height = 20,
    res = 300,
    units = "in")

SparccP_plot <- SparccP %>% ggplot(aes(x = Var2,
                                       y = Var1, 
                                       fill = cor)) + 
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) +
  
  geom_point(data = sparccOkP, shape = "*", size = 15, color = "red")

SparccP_plot

dev.off()
png 
  2
